pattern: The shortest unlocking string is a de Bruijn sequence: an Eulerian-style walk that visits every length-n digit combination exactly once, built greedily by always appending the smallest digit that yields an unseen window.
hints: Every length-n combination must appear as a contiguous window, and the minimum total length is k^n plus n-1 overlapping leading digits.
Start from a string of n repeated zeros, then repeatedly try to extend by one digit so the new trailing n-window has not been used before.
A greedy depth-first construction that always tries digits in a fixed order and records each visited window produces a valid de Bruijn sequence covering all combinations.

edge_inputs.description: single digit per password, binary alphabet
single password slot with full decimal alphabet

stress.description: random valid (n,k) pairs kept within the size cap k^n <= 4096
